text.title: Kubernetes Security Hardening Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules plus a skip-clause for `Kubernetes Security Hardening`. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Production namespaces MUST be labelled with pod-security.kubernetes.io/enforce=restricted; privileged/baseline enforce is forbidden in prod.
rule.rationale: Restricted profile blocks the dominant container-escape vectors (root, hostNetwork, hostPath).

rule.statement: Every production namespace MUST have a default-deny NetworkPolicy with explicit allow rules; no NetworkPolicy means implicit allow-all.
rule.rationale: Without NetworkPolicy any pod can reach any pod; lateral movement is trivial.

rule.statement: Container securityContext.capabilities MUST drop ALL and add only the minimal set required.
rule.rationale: Default capabilities (NET_RAW, etc.) expand attack surface unnecessarily.

rule.statement: Container securityContext.readOnlyRootFilesystem MUST be true; writable rootfs is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Read-only rootfs blocks the most common payload-write step of post-exploit chains.

rule.statement: Production clusters MUST enforce that images are signed (Cosign / Notary) via a policy engine; unsigned images are rejected at admission.
rule.rationale: Unsigned images can be swapped via registry compromise; signature verification closes that vector.

rule.statement: If Applies If preconditions fail OR any Skip If trigger fires, do not run this methodology.
